originalTitle: Nvidia says its AI data center design runs hotter to use a lot less water
summary: Public pushback against data centers has emphasized their water and energy consumption, and now Nvidia is highlighting its claim that the Rubin generation reference design for a fully liquid-cooled data center has "eliminated massive amounts of power usage and pretty much all water usage." Still, it doesn't address all of the concerns around AI data […]
content: Public pushback against data centers has emphasized their water and energy consumption, and now Nvidia is highlighting its claim that the Rubin generation reference design for a fully liquid-cooled data center has “eliminated massive amounts of power usage and pretty much all water usage.” Still, it doesn’t address all of the concerns around AI data centers, including during their construction, and for the power generation requirements of the massive facilities. Also, as Gizmodo points out, Nvidia’s blog post doesn’t mention the cost of building this style of data center vs. one using less efficient air cooling, but claims that “every cloud provider and data center operator building for [Rubin] is making the transition.” The efficiency gains are partly due to running AI servers hotter, as high as 113 degrees Fahrenheit (45 degrees Celsius). In a recent report, Amazon similarly touted higher heat tolerances as part of making its mostly air-cooled data centers more efficient. With Nvidia’s system, “heat is captured directly at the chip and transported through liquid loops operating at much higher temperatures, allowing outdoor dry coolers to reject heat efficiently for much of the year,” with much more flexibility when it comes to the ambient air temperature.  According to Nvidia’s head of sustainability, Josh Parker, the reference design takes water use “from roughly 2.6 million gallons per megawatt per year for conventional cooling-tower-based systems to near zero — up to a 100 percent reduction.”
chineseTitle: 英伟达表示，其 AI 数据中心设计运行温度更高，可减少用水量
chineseSummary: 公众对数据中心的反对强调了它们的水和能源消耗，现在英伟达强调其声称，全液冷数据中心的 Rubin 一代参考设计已经“消除了大量的电力使用和几乎所有的水使用”。尽管如此，它并没有解决围绕人工智能数据的所有担忧 […]
chineseContent: 公众对数据中心的反对强调了它们的水和能源消耗，现在 Nvidia 强调其声称，全液冷数据中心的 Rubin 一代参考设计已经“消除了大量的电力消耗和几乎所有的水消耗”。尽管如此，它并没有解决围绕人工智能数据中心的所有问题，包括在其建设过程中以及大型设施的发电要求。此外，正如 Gizmodo 指出的那样，Nvidia 的博客文章没有提及构建这种类型的数据中心与使用效率较低的空气冷却的数据中心的成本，但声称“为 [Rubin] 构建的每个云提供商和数据中心运营商都在进行转型。”效率提升的部分原因是人工智能服务器运行温度更高，高达 113 华氏度（45 摄氏度）。在最近的一份报告中，亚马逊同样吹捧更高的耐热性，作为提高其大部分风冷数据中心效率的一部分。借助 Nvidia 的系统，“热量直接在芯片上捕获，并通过在更高温度下运行的液体回路传输，使室外干式冷却器能够在一年中的大部分时间里有效地排出热量”，在环境空气温度方面具有更大的灵活性。  据 Nvidia 可持续发展主管 Josh Parker 称，该参考设计“将传统冷却塔系统的每年每兆瓦用水量约 260 万加仑降至接近于零，最多可减少 100%”。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
